One Link
How it works Features Security Share One Get One Link
Transparency

What we know. What we don't.

One Link is software you run. The architecture was designed so that the smallest possible amount of information passes through anything we operate. This page is the dated, public record of what that means.

Warrant canary

Last verified 2026-05-17.

As of the date above, the One Link contributors have received zero government requests of any kind related to this project, its source repositories, its relay infrastructure, or its website. We have issued zero National Security Letters or equivalent gag orders, because no such instrument has been served.

This page is updated on a monthly cadence. The disappearance or expiration of this statement is itself the signal.

The statement is signed and committed to the public source repository at every refresh, so historical attestations are tamper-evident via git log.

// canary statement, plain
date:               2026-05-17
requests_received:  0
gag_orders_received: 0
subpoenas_received: 0
NSLs_received:      0
next_refresh:       2026-06-17

// signed in commit history
What the architecture leaves out of our reach

The smallest possible surface.

Message content

End-to-end encrypted with post-quantum-hybrid keys. The cleartext never reaches a relay, never reaches a server, never reaches us. There is no plaintext for any party other than the peers to hand over.

Account identifiers

One Link uses opaque rotating peer IDs. There is no email, phone number, or username database to subpoena. Recognition happens device to device through pairing.

Social graph

Pairings are stored on the devices that hold them, not on a server. The aggregate "who knows who" graph that messengers typically retain does not exist anywhere we operate.

Relay logs

Relays are designed to be stateless forwarders. They route sealed envelopes by topology, not by identity. They do not retain access logs across restarts. Anyone can run one.

Location

The live mesh page shows approximate continental region only, derived from relay topology. No precise location of any peer is held anywhere we control.

Payment records

One Link is free. There is no subscription, no purchase history, no billing address. There is nothing to compel.

What this site does involve us in

The honest list.

For full visibility, here is exactly what the website surface (this domain, not the One Link daemon) processes:

Cloudflare as host

weareone-link.org runs on Cloudflare Workers. Cloudflare sees the IP of every request, as any CDN does. We have configured the worker to override Cloudflare's default error-reporting telemetry headers so no NEL beacons fire from your browser.

Anonymous presence overlay

The "N here right now" pill uses a Cloudflare Durable Object that holds nothing but a per-session random ID for the lifetime of your tab. The ID rotates every visit. No identifying data is stored.

Encrypted share buffer

/share/ accepts ciphertext only. The key lives in the URL fragment and never reaches the server. The ciphertext is deleted at first download or after 24 hours, whichever comes first.

No tracking, no analytics

Zero cookies. Zero analytics. Zero third-party scripts. Zero ad networks. Zero tracking pixels. This is enforced by the Content-Security-Policy that ships with every page.

Cadence

Refreshed monthly. Always.

If this page falls behind, that is itself a signal. Subscribe to the source repository for atomic notifications when this file changes.

Source: commit history for this page.

One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
